name: python-daemon (UNIX Daemon Process Library)

performance.notes: Fork overhead at daemon start; negligible after

agent_readiness.notes: PEP 3143 UNIX daemon process library. import daemon. import lockfile. with daemon.DaemonContext(pidfile=lockfile.FileLock('/var/run/myapp.pid'), stdout=open('/var/log/myapp.out', 'w+'), stderr=open('/var/log/myapp.err', 'w+'), working_directory='/'): main_loop(). DaemonContext parameters: uid, gid, umask, working_directory, chroot_directory, files_preserve, pidfile, stdin, stdout, stderr, signal_map. signal_map: {signal.SIGTERM: shutdown, signal.SIGHUP: reload}. DaemonContext.open() / DaemonContext.close(). Implements double-fork daemonization per Stevens APUE.

gotchas: Double-fork daemonization closes all file descriptors — save handles in files_preserve
Niche use case — systemd and Docker have replaced most daemon needs
No type annotations — limited IDE support
best_when: Daemonizing Python processes on Linux without systemd
Legacy UNIX deployments requiring PID file management
When double-fork daemonization per APUE spec is required
avoid_when: Modern Linux with systemd — use systemd unit files instead
Docker/container environments — process management is handled by container runtime
When supervisor is already managing processes
summary: python-daemon implements PEP 3143 UNIX daemon creation. with DaemonContext(pidfile=..., stdout=..., signal_map=...): main_loop(). Double-fork daemonization. PID file management. Apache-2.0 by Ben Finney. Niche use case — systemd and Docker have largely replaced this. files_preserve needed for open handles. UNIX only. No type annotations. Best for legacy UNIX daemonization without systemd.
